articles.title: Brickbat: Pawned Off
articles.content:
印第安纳州新芝加哥市警察局局长厄尔·梅奥(Earl Mayo)面临刑事指控，调查人员称他将一把储存在刑事案件中的手枪卖给了当铺。当当局发现枪支失踪后，检察官称梅奥试图让另一名警官将其买回。据称，梅奥还要求两名警官前往他家中，其中一人去取回武器，另一人去取回他的类固醇药物，以避免联邦调查人员发现这些物品。梅奥被指控犯有盗窃罪、职务不当行为、妨碍司法罪以及非法持有合成类固醇罪。

文章砖块： pawned off首次发表于Reason.com。
articles.summary:
Police Chief Earl Mayo of New Chicago, Indiana, is facing criminal charges after investigators say he sold a handgun to a pawn shop that had been stored as evidence in a criminal case. When authorities discovered the gun was missing, prosecutors say Mayo tried to have another officer buy it back. Mayo also allegedly asked two officers go to go his home, one to retrieve weapons and the other to retrieve his steroids, before federal investigators could find them. Mayo is charged with theft, official misconduct, obstruction, and unlawful possession of anabolic steroids.

The post Brickbat: Pawned Off appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: My New Article Making "The Case Against Mass Deportation"
articles.content:
今日，法治协会在其“制衡”子栏目上发表了我撰写的《反对大规模驱逐的理由》一文。以下是文章节选：

自唐纳德·特朗普于2025年1月重返白宫以来，美国的移民执法和驱逐行动变得比几十年来甚至历史上任何时候都更加残酷和压迫性——受害者既包括移民，也包括许多美国公民。遏制这些滥用行为的最佳方式是总体上减少驱逐。

自特朗普2025年1月重返白宫以来，美国移民与海关执法局（ICE）及其他联邦执法人员已至少导致三名美国公民死亡（其中两名在明尼苏达州，一名在得克萨斯州），多人受伤，并非法拘留了数百人，通常是因为将他们误认为非法移民。根据ProPublica的调查，截至2025年10月，已发现约170起非法拘留美国公民的案例。但这一数字几乎可以肯定是严重低估的。

ICE及其他机构还广泛使用种族外貌特征进行筛选（即种族画像），导致许多亚裔、非裔或拉丁裔美国人，或仅因外貌特征而被误认为可能为非法移民的人遭到拘留和骚扰。ICE在种族和族裔方面的广泛画像行为，从洛杉矶县在一项禁止使用此类策略的联邦法院命令后，移民逮捕数量下降了66%这一事实中可见一斑（尽管该裁决最终因程序性原因被最高法院叫停）。主张政府应无视肤色的保守派人士，不能在移民执法问题上随意做出例外处理。

ICE拘留设施的条件往往极其恶劣，普遍表现为过度拥挤、食物不足以及拒绝提供基本医疗。自本财年10月开始以来，已有创纪录的29人在ICE拘留中死亡，尽管本财年才刚刚过半。这些暴行和对公民自由的侵犯，因本届政府大幅扩编ICE人员（包括雇佣许多资质不足的人员）以及实施驱逐配额制度（鼓励执法人员在缺乏证据甚至毫无证据的情况下任意逮捕）而进一步加剧。非法的ICE拘留事件频发，法院已在相关拘留案件中对政府作出不利裁决约11,500次，其中包括数千项由共和党任命的法官所做出的裁决。

驱逐体系中的种种滥用行为在特朗普政策推动下显著增加。但即使在较为传统的总统执政时期，该体系也依然残酷而不公正。美国公民被非法拘留和驱逐的现象早在特朗普之前就已存在。西北大学政治学教授杰奎琳·史蒂文斯（Jacqueline Stevens）估计，从2003年到2010年，联邦政府至少拘留或驱逐了超过20,000名美国公民。种族画像同样并非仅存在于特朗普时代。

归根结底，若想驱逐美国境内约1370万非法移民中的较大比例，而不对大量人员实施缺乏正当程序的逮捕和拘留，这在实际操作中是不可能的。

最终，唯一能够终结或大幅减少这种暴行的方式，就是从根本上大幅减少驱逐行为本身。实现这一目标有多种可能的改革路径……

文章的其余部分概述了多种潜在的改革方案，例如……

本文最初发表于Reason.com。
articles.summary:
Today, the Society for the Rule of Law published my article on "The Case Against Mass Deportation," on its Checks and Balances substack. Here is an excerpt:

Since Donald Trump returned to the White House in January 2025, U.S. immigration enforcement and deportation operations have become more cruel and oppressive than they have been in many decades, if not ever - with victims including both immigrants and many US citizens. The best approach to curbing these abuses is cutting back on deportations more generally.

Since Trump returned to office in January 2025, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and other federal officers have killed at least three U.S. citizens (two in Minnesota and one in Texas), wounded numerous others, and detained hundreds illegally, usually after mistaking them for illegal migrants. ProPublica found some 170 cases of illegal detention of citizens through October 2025. But that is almost certainly a severe underestimate.

ICE and other agencies also make extensive use of racial profiling, which leads to detention and harassment of numerous U.S. citizens who are Asian, black, or Hispanic, or look like they might be and thus potentially suspect. The enormous extent of racial and ethnic profiling by ICE is shown by the fact that immigration arrests in Los Angeles County declined by 66 percent after a federal court order barring the use of such tactics (the ruling was eventually blocked by the Supreme Court, perhaps for procedural reasons). Conservatives and others who favor color-blind government cannot make an arbitrary exception for immigration enforcement.

Conditions in ICE detention facilities are often horrific, routinely featuring overcrowding, inadequate food and denial of essential medical treatment. Since the start of the present fiscal year in October, a record 29 people have died in ICE detention, even though the fiscal year is only about half over. The brutality and violations of civil liberties have been exacerbated by the administration's massive expansion of the number of ICE personnel (including hiring many poorly qualified people), and by its imposition of deportation quotas incentivizing arbitrary arrests with little or no proof. There have been so many illegal ICE detentions that courts have ruled against the administration in detention cases some 11,500 times, including thousands of decisions issued by Republican-appointed judges….

The abuses of the deportation system have increased significantly thanks to Trump's policies. But the system is cruel and unjust even under more conventional presidents. Illegal detention and deportation of U.S. citizens long predates Trump. Northwestern University political scientist Jacqueline Stevens estimated that the federal government detained or deported more than 20,000 U.S. citizens from 2003 to 2010…. Racial profiling is also not unique to the Trump era….

Ultimately, it is impossible to deport any large proportion of the estimated 13.7 million undocumented immigrants in the United States without arresting and detaining many people with little or no due process….

Ultimately, the only way to end or greatly reduce that brutality is to radically scale back deportation itself. There are a variety of ways to do that…..

The rest of the piece outlines a variety of potential reforms, such as these.

 

The post My New Article Making "The Case Against Mass Deportation" appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: Court Upholds Dismissal of U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary Officer for "Crass Statements on LinkedIn" "in Uniform"
articles.content:
在今天由第七巡回上诉法院法官迈克尔·斯库德（Michael Scudder）裁决的Wenzler诉美国海岸警卫队案中，法官Amy St. Eve和Joshua Kolar参与了裁决：

詹姆斯·温泽尔（James Wenzler）在担任海岸警卫队辅助部队（Coast Guard Auxiliary）成员期间，在领英（LinkedIn）上发布了一系列粗俗言论，并在帖子中展示了自己身穿制服的照片。辅助部队对此展开调查，要求他停止此类行为，并在温泽尔未听从警告后将其从组织中除名……

温泽尔于2007年加入海岸警卫队辅助部队。多年来，他担任过多个职位，最终升任副舰队指挥官。他在领英上公开了自己与该组织的关系，其个人资料中展示了自己身穿制服的照片，并称自己是辅助部队人力资源部门的主管。

2022年5月，一名公众成员向辅助部队投诉温泽尔在领英上的帖子。其中一篇帖子指责某些最高法院大法官有种族主义倾向，另一篇则对女童子军发表了粗俗评论。

以下是来自地方法院意见的帖子详情：

第一条帖子是对祝贺凯坦吉·布朗·杰克逊（Ketanji Brown Jackson）大法官的帖子的回应。温泽尔的回应写道：“又一个种族主义者加入了法院，与种族主义者索托马约尔（Sotomayor）和卡根（Kagan）为伍。干得好，美国！”第二条帖子是对一位女童子军写信给编辑抱怨某项关于男女童子军的公告（该作者认为该公告有性别歧视）的帖子的回应。温泽尔的回应写道：“如果你只是在证明自己只是在玩乐，那你就只是在玩乐。认为某件事有性别歧视，恰恰说明你才是性别歧视者。也许女童子军应该真正做点什么，但可悲的是，她们只是卖饼干。”

回到第七巡回法院的意见：

辅助部队的调查导致地区司令哈维·兰德尔（Harvey Randall）向温泽尔发出一封警告信。信中要求温泽尔从社交媒体上删除自己身穿制服的照片，并删除任何提及在辅助部队中担任职位的内容。信中还要求温泽尔确认遵守该指示。

温泽尔未予遵守。事实上，2022年7月15日，他给地区司令兰德尔发邮件称：“我不同意你那封虚假的警告信，我将对你提出投诉，控告你因我是白人而对我进行种族主义和偏见行为。我认为你的行为令人不齿。”

温泽尔随后依然我行我素。2022年8月，辅助部队发现他的领英个人资料仍展示其身穿制服的照片，并将其列为人力资源部主管。辅助部队还发现了其他一些敏感且侮辱性的帖子。例如，温泽尔曾讽刺刚刚被诊断出癌症的西北大学当选校长在威斯康星大学麦迪逊分校（她的前雇主）“干得很糟糕”，并“最终得到了她在那里所作所为的现实后果”。一位公众成员看到该帖子后在评论中表示不满，质疑辅助部队是否真的让温泽尔负责人力资源工作——实质上是在质疑他是否适合担任领导职务。

当辅助部队跟进时，温泽尔坚持己见。他确认自己无意遵守辅助部队的社交媒体指示。因此，辅助部队暂停了他的职务并启动了正式的纪律程序，最终导致海岸警卫队将温泽尔除名……

法院维持了该除名决定：

“在某些情况下，第一修正案保护公职人员作为公民就公共事务发表言论的权利。”无论发言者是政府组织的志愿者还是有薪雇员，我们的分析都相同……为了确定言论是否受到宪法保护，我们采用两步的Connick/Pickering测试。首先，我们判断该言论是否涉及公共事务，而非纯粹的个人利益。如果是，则需在发言者“就公共事务发表评论的利益”与政府“促进其雇员履行公共服务效率的利益”之间进行权衡……

即使我们接受温泽尔的言论涉及公共事务，我们也只关注双方利益的平衡。根据我们面前的事实，辅助部队对“同事间的纪律或和谐”以及持续的“公众信心”的需求占主导地位。

组织的性质决定了我们在判断其成员言论是否会对其组织使命产生不利影响时应给予的尊重程度。双方都引用了我们判例中的语言，指出某些组织被认定为“准军事组织”，因此在这一评估中应得到更多尊重。这些案件考虑了执法机构、矫正中心等组织的自由裁量权。我们确实尊重这些类型组织对其安全和纪律风险的“自身评估”。但这种尊重来自于组织的性质及其使命，而不是来自于是否符合“准军事”标签的单独测试……

我们确信，当辅助部队成员身穿制服并以组织成员身份示人时，辅助部队在评估和回应其成员言论方面应得到尊重……“出于指挥、控制和行政目的”，国会赋予了辅助部队一个类似军事的等级结构，包括“全国董事会和工作人员……地区、区域、分区、舰队及其他组织单位”。

辅助部队的潜在任务非常重大。从宪法角度看，总统作为总司令，可指示海岸警卫队司令请求并授权辅助部队协助。从法律角度看，辅助部队可“协助海岸警卫队履行任何法律授权的职能、权力、职责、角色、使命或行动”。这包括在满足某些前提条件时“对水道进行巡逻”。

虽然辅助人员不得“从事直接执法或军事任务”或“执行限制区域”，但他们“可就公众遵守限制区域提供建议”。辅助人员还可作为训练演习中的“非武装对抗力量”，并“收集信息和数据，用于制定海岸警卫队、州和地方政府的应急计划”。国会进一步认识到某些任务可能使辅助人员面临风险，因此为那些“在执行海岸警卫队指派的任何职责时因身体伤害而受伤或死亡”的人员提供了福利。

就温泽尔而言，他将辅助部队视为一个更有限的组织，即一个志愿者“非军事组织”，其成员选举自己的领导，且不得携带武器。他告诉我们，辅助部队不过是一个“政府资助的兄弟会”。我们不能同意。根据法律，国会设立了辅助部队，并使其成为海岸警卫队的一部分，因此至少间接地对海岸警卫队司令负责。

温泽尔还辩称，由于辅助部队缺乏强制成员服从的手段，因此在规范其言论方面不应得到尊重。对此，我们同样不同意。即使在美国实际的武装部队中，获得领导特权也始终需要下属的认同。鉴于其法律制度和相关法规，我们确信辅助部队在其成员言论如何影响其使命的合理判断方面应得到尊重……

根据我们面前无可争议的事实，辅助部队可以合理地认定温泽尔的言论和行为将对其组织及其声誉造成不利影响。他担任领导职务，他树立的榜样至关重要。辅助部队可以合理地预期，其他辅助人员在看到他的贬低性评论和对他人攻击后，将不太愿意与他合作，或至少不会与他良好合作……

“只要预测合理，公共雇主可基于潜在干扰采取行动。”……温泽尔的言论至少引起了两名公众成员的警觉，他们通知了辅助部队。根据这一记录，辅助部队可以合理地担心对其声誉的负面影响，进而影响其招募和留任。从长远来看，其服务公众的能力可能会受到影响，或者至少辅助部队可以合理地相信会如此。

美国检察官办公室（威斯康星西部区）的罗伯特·英-蔡·乔伊特（Robert Ying-Tsai Joynt）和莱斯利·K·赫尔杰（Leslie K. Herje）代表被告。

本文《法院维持对美国海岸警卫队辅助部队官员因“在制服状态下在领英上发表粗俗言论”的除名裁决》首次发表于Reason.com。
articles.summary:
From Wenzler v. U.S. Coast Guard, decided today by Seventh Circuit Judge Michael Scudder, joined by Judges Amy St. Eve and Joshua Kolar:

James Wenzler posted a series of crass statements on LinkedIn while serving as a member of the Coast Guard Auxiliary and depicting himself in uniform. The Auxiliary investigated, told him to stop, and removed him from its ranks after he failed to heed its warning….

James Wenzler joined the Coast Guard Auxiliary in 2007. He held various positions over the years, eventually becoming a Vice Flotilla Commander. Wenzler publicized his affiliation with the organization on LinkedIn. His profile depicted him in uniform and represented that he served as the Auxiliary's Branch Chief for Human Resources.

In May 2022, a member of the public complained to the Auxiliary about Wenzler's LinkedIn posts. One of the posts accused certain Supreme Court Justices of being racist, and another included a crude remark about the Girl Scouts.

Here are the details on the posts, from the district court opinion:

The first [post] was in response to a post congratulating Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson. Wenzler's responsive post read: "Another racist makes the court to join racist Sotom[a]yor and Kag[a]n. Great job America!" The second post was in response to a post commending a girl scout for writing a letter to the editor to complain about an announcement regarding boy and girl scouts that the writer deemed sexist. Wenzler's responsive post read: "Well if you are proving you are just having fun, then you are. To find something sexist is to show you are the sexist. Perhaps the Girl Scouts should actually accomplish something, but alas they just sell cookies."

Back to the Seventh Circuit opinion:

The Auxiliary's investigation resulted in District Commodore Harvey Randall issuing Wenzler a letter of caution. The letter directed Wenzler to remove from social media any photos of himself wearing his uniform and to delete any reference to positions in the Auxiliary. It also instructed Wenzler to confirm compliance with the directive.

Wenzler failed to comply. Indeed, on July 15, he emailed District Commodore Randall, stating, "I disagree with your fake Letter of Caution, and am going to file a complaint against you for your racist and bigoted action against me because I am White. I find your behavior reprehensible."

Wenzler then stayed the course. In August 2022, the Auxiliary found that his LinkedIn profile still depicted him in uniform and listed him as a Branch Chief in the Human Resources Directorate. The Auxiliary also discovered additional insensitive and insulting posts. For example, Wenzler had quipped that the President-elect of Northwestern University, who had just been diagnosed with cancer, did a "horrible job" at the University of Wisconsin—Madison, her former employer, and "end[ed] up with the physical results of what she was" there. A member of the public saw the post and reacted negatively, asking in the comments if the Auxiliary really had put Wenzler in charge of human resources—essentially questioning whether he was fit to serve as a leader.

When the Auxiliary followed up, Wenzler doubled down. He confirmed that he had no intention of adhering to the Auxiliary's social media directive. So the Auxiliary suspended him and began a formal disciplinary process, which resulted in the Coast Guard disenrolling Wenzler….

The court upheld the dismissal:

"[T]he First Amendment protects a public employee's right, in certain circumstances, to speak as a citizen addressing matters of public concern." Our analysis is the same whether the speaker is a volunteer for a government organization or a paid employee…. To determine whether speech is constitutionally protected, we apply the two-step Connick/Pickering test. First we ask whether the speech in question involved a matter of public concern, as opposed to a purely personal interest. If so, we balance the speaker's interest "in commenting upon matters of public concern" against the government's interest "in promoting the efficiency of the public services it performs through its employees." …

Accepting that Wenzler's speech touched on matters of public concern, we focus only on balancing the parties' interests. On the facts before us, the Auxiliary's need for "discipline or harmony among co-workers" and continued "public confidence" dominate.

The nature of an organization informs the deference we give in determining whether its employees' speech will deleteriously impact the organizational mission. The parties have latched onto language in our precedent identifying certain organizations as "paramilitary" and thus entitled to greater deference in this assessment. These cases considered the discretion afforded to law enforcement agencies, correctional centers, and the like. And indeed we deferred to those types of organizations' "own assessment of the risks to security and discipline." But the deference in reasonably determining what type of conduct is detrimental comes from the nature of an organization and its mission. It does not stem from any separate test for whether an organization qualifies under a "paramilitary" label….

We are convinced that the Coast Guard Auxiliary deserves deference in assessing and responding to its members' speech when they hold themselves out as members of the organization while wearing its uniform…. "For command, control, and administrative purposes," Congress gave the Auxiliary a military-like hierarchy consisting of "a national board and staff …, districts, regions, divisions, flotillas, and other organizational elements and units." …

The Auxiliary's possible missions are substantial. As a constitutional matter, the President, as Commander in Chief, may direct the Commandant to request and authorize assistance from the Auxiliary. As a statutory matter, the Auxiliary may "assist the Coast Guard as authorized by the Commandant, in performing any Coast Guard function, power, duty, role, mission, or operation authorized by law." This includes the ability to "conduct a patrol of a waterway" if certain preconditions are met.

And while Auxiliarists may not "engage in direct law enforcement or military missions" or "enforce limited access areas," they "may advise the public regarding compliance with [a] limited access area." Auxiliarists may also serve as "unarmed opposition forces" during training exercises as well as "gather information and data for the development of Coast Guard, State, and local government contingency plans." Congress further recognized that some missions could place Auxiliarists at risk and therefore provided benefits to those who are "physically injured or die[ ] as a result of physical injury incurred while performing any duty" assigned to them by the Coast Guard.

For his part, Wenzler sees the Auxiliary in more limited terms, as only a volunteer "nonmilitary organization" where members elect their own leadership and may not carry weapons. He tells us that the Auxiliary is nothing more than a "government-sponsored fraternity." We cannot agree. By statute, Congress established the Auxiliary and made it a component of the Coast Guard and thus, at least indirectly, answerable to the Commandant of the Coast Guard.

Wenzler also contends that because the Auxiliary lacks the means to compel obedience from its members it should receive no deference in policing their speech. Here, too, we disagree. Even in the actual armed forces of the United States, earning the privilege to lead has always required buy-in from subordinates. Given its statutory scheme and enabling regulations, we are convinced that the Auxiliary deserves deference in its reasonable determinations of how its members' speech will impact its mission….

Given the uncontested facts before us, the Auxiliary could have reasonably determined that Wenzler's speech and actions would be detrimental to the Auxiliary and its reputation. He served in a leadership role and the example he set mattered. The Auxiliary could have reasonably expected that other Auxiliarists would be less likely to work with Wenzler, or at least to work well with him, following his derogatory comments and attacks on others….

"[A] public employer may act based on potential disruption so long as its predictions are reasonable." … Wenzler's statements alarmed at least two members of the public enough to notify the Auxiliary. On this record, the Auxiliary could have been justifiably concerned about negative impacts to its reputation and, in turn, its recruiting and retention. In the long run, its ability to serve the public might suffer, or at least the Auxiliary could have reasonably believed so.

Robert Ying-Tsai Joynt and Leslie K. Herje of the U.S. Attorney's Office (W.D. Wis.) represent defendants.

The post Court Upholds Dismissal of U.S. Coast Guard Auxiliary Officer for "Crass Statements on LinkedIn" "in Uniform" appeared first on Reason.com.

articles.title: Why Socialism Won't Deliver Government Efficiency
articles.content:
本周，《理性》杂志的编辑彼得·苏德曼、凯瑟琳·曼古-沃德、尼克·吉列皮和马特·韦尔奇讨论了纽约市长佐赫兰·马姆达尼（Zohran Mamdani）新成立的政府效率委员会（COGE）。该小组审视了马姆达尼关于官僚体制阻碍纽约提供住房、交通和其他公共服务的说法，同时质疑该委员会究竟是真正改革的举措，还是仅仅是对城市现有政治优先事项的重新包装。他们还探讨了马姆达尼对效率修辞的拥抱所反映出的民主党现状，以及人们日益认识到政府往往难以兑现承诺。

接下来，编辑们讨论了前副总统迈克·彭斯（Mike Pence）声称“民主党已经失去理智”的言论，并审视了民主党持续吸引极端候选人的现象，从缅因州参议员候选人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（Graham Platner）到加州尚未明朗的州长竞选。随后，小组关注了持续悬而未决的伊朗冲突，接着辩论了一项为老年人免除财产税的提案，并探讨这一趋势反映出的税收优惠日益向特定群体倾斜的现象。最后，编辑们回答了一位听众的提问，探讨已故参议员约翰·麦凯恩（John McCain，共和党-亚利桑那州）对综合格斗（MMA）的反对是否影响了最终使UFC活动走进白宫草坪的文化力量。

0:00—政府效率委员会  
14:04—迈克·彭斯对民主党的评论  
22:21—格雷厄姆·普拉特纳与民主党候选人质量  
30:19—伊朗战争更新  
33:01—听众提问：白宫UFC活动  
42:30—老年人是否应缴纳财产税？  
50:05—本周文化推荐  

相关文章包括：  
“埃隆搞了DOGE，现在马姆达尼在尝试COGE”，作者：梅根·奥罗克（Megan O'Rourke）  
“马姆达尼‘平衡’纽约市预算——靠的是奥尔巴尼的救助”，作者：乔·兰开斯特（Joe Lancaster）  
“对市场原则的信心”，作者：迈克·彭斯  
“迈克·彭斯理性（但可能注定失败）的国家债务解决方案”，作者：埃里克·伯姆（Eric Boehm）  
“今天迈克·彭斯可能发现，他对选举的所有拖延和模糊处理最终换来的只是牺牲了自己的正直”，作者：雅各布·萨勒姆（Jacob Sullum）  
“肯·帕克斯顿在初选中的胜利显示特朗普的怨恨如何损害其政党的利益”，作者：雅各布·萨勒姆  
“别纹纳粹纹身或赞美希特勒：给新政治家的建议”，作者：罗比·索夫（Robby Soave）  
“渗入主流”，作者：格雷格·比托（Greg Beato）  
“政府持续打压综合格斗”，作者：凯瑞·豪利（Kerry Howley）  
“UFC：一人眼中的残酷表演，另一人眼中的伊甸园”，作者：A·巴顿·欣克尔（A. Barton Hinkle）  
“为老年人提供财产税减免”，作者：埃里克·伯姆  
“婴儿潮一代是历史上最富有的一代。为什么政客们还在不断为他们花钱？”，作者：埃里克·伯姆  
“废除财产税？”，作者：克里斯蒂安·布里施吉（Christian Britschgi）  
“海伦·刘易斯：天才的阴暗面”，作者：尼克·吉列皮  

《为什么社会主义无法带来政府效率》一文最初发表于Reason.com。
articles.summary:
This week, editors Peter Suderman, Katherine Mangu-Ward, Nick Gillespie, and Matt Welch discuss New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new Commission on Government Efficiency (COGE). The panel examines Mamdani's claim that bureaucracy is preventing New York from delivering housing, transit, and other services, while questioning whether the commission represents a serious effort at reform or simply a rebranding of the city's existing political priorities. They also consider what Mamdani's embrace of efficiency rhetoric says about the state of the Democratic Party and the growing recognition that government often struggles to deliver on its promises.

Next, the editors discuss former Vice President Mike Pence's claim that "Democrats have lost their mind" and examine the Democratic Party's continuing attraction to polarizing candidates, from Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner to California's unsettled gubernatorial race. The panel then checks in on the endlessly unresolved Iran conflict, before debating a proposal to eliminate property taxes for senior citizens and what it reveals about the growing trend toward narrowly targeted tax breaks. Finally, the editors answer a listener's question about whether late Sen. John McCain's (R–Ariz.) opposition to mixed martial arts helped shape the cultural forces that eventually brought the UFC to the White House lawn.

 

0:00—The Commission on Government Efficiency

14:04—Mike Pence's comments on Democratic Party

22:21—Graham Platner and Democratic candidate quality

30:19—Iran war update

33:01—Listener question on the White House UFC event

42:30—Should seniors pay property tax?

50:05—Weekly cultural recommendations

 

Mentioned in the podcast:

"Elon Did DOGE. Now Mamdani Is Trying COGE," by Megan O'Rourke

"Mamdani 'Balanced' New York City's Budget—With a Bailout From Albany," by Joe Lancaster

"Confidence in Market Principles," by Mike Pence

"Mike Pence's Sensible (and Probably Doomed) Plan To Fix the National Debt," by Eric Boehm

"Today Mike Pence May Find That All His Temporizing and Obfuscation About the Election Sacrificed His Integrity for Nothing," by Jacob Sullum

"Ken Paxton's Primary Victory Shows How Trump's Grudges Undermine His Party's Interests," by Jacob Sullum

"Don't Get a Nazi Tattoo or Praise Hitler: Advice for New Politicians," by Robby Soave

"Bleeding into the Mainstream," by Greg Beato

"Government's Ongoing Effort to Shutter Mixed Martial Arts," by Kerry Howley

"UFC: One Man's Brutal Spectacle Is Another's Eden," by A. Barton Hinkle

"Stop Giving Property Tax Breaks to Senior Citizens," by Eric Boehm

"Baby Boomers Are the Richest Generation in History. Why Do Politicians Keep Spending More on Them?" by Eric Boehm

"Abolish Property Taxes?" by Christian Britschgi

"Helen Lewis: The Dark Side of Genius," by Nick Gillespie

The post Why Socialism Won't Deliver Government Efficiency appeared first on Reason.com.
